prevalence.value: common seasonal illness; highest in children 6 months to 3 years

vulnerablePopulations: children with limited access to emergency care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with barking cough and stridor; rule out epiglottitis and foreign body

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with steroids; severe cases require epinephrine and observation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care and emergency departments; limited imaging
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dexamethasone; nebulized epinephrine for moderate-severe cases; supportive care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access to timely emergency care in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost effective therapy and expanding pediatric emergency services
